data.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (3/N).

Q5 — MCP config/trust: DEDICATED ALLOWLIST + SECRETS, mirroring the prompt two-tier model. rebar SHIPS a default MCP allowlist; projects ADD to it via their own .rebar/ config and store secrets in PROJECT config (never inline). Resolution mirrors prompts: built-in ⊕ project, project-name-wins on collision. A workflow may call only an allow-listed server.tool; else lint/runtime refusal (allowlist IS the trust boundary — Flowise RCE CVE-2025-59528 lesson). Gated by a new REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_MCP_CALL-style env flag, default off. Allowlist entry: name -> {transport stdio|http, command|url, allowed_tools[]}; creds via ${{ secrets.x }}.

Q6 — Prompt write-back location: AUTO-DETECT EDITABLE REBAR CHECKOUT. On Save, detect a writable nava-rebar source tree (pyproject [project].name=='nava-rebar' AND real writable src/rebar/llm/... path). If YES => author/edit the BUILT-IN packaged .md directly (developing rebar). If NO (client project / installed wheel, site-packages read-only) => write project override .rebar/prompts/<id>.md, never touching packaged files; refuse packaged writes when not writable. Matches epic policy with a concrete testable signal. Item-4 requires front-matter-preserving write + round-trip corruption tests.

Q7 — MCP tool discovery for palette: CACHED MANIFEST + EXPLICIT REFRESH. rebar introspects a server once (tools/list), caches tool list + inputSchemas to .rebar/mcp-tools/<server>.json; editor palette reads the CACHE (offline-capable, git-reviewable, deterministic). Explicit 'rebar mcp refresh <server>' (CLI + editor button) re-introspects. Run-time re-validates args vs the live inputSchema to catch drift. Chosen over live-at-edit (online-dependent, no palette when server down) and auto-TTL (unpredictable blocking + cache churn in git).
